title: Multi-stage Dockerfile building admin panel and server into one image
description: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile that builds the Vite-bundled admin panel and the Node.js/tRPC server, producing a single production image that serves both from one process/container, per the 'container único' principle.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile has separate build stages for `apps/admin` (Vite build) and `apps/server` (TypeScript build)
Final stage is a slim Node.js runtime image containing only production dependencies and build output
Final image serves the admin panel's static build output from the server process (no separate nginx/static server needed)
Final image exposes `PORT` and respects `DATABASE_PATH` for the SQLite volume mount point
`docker build .` completes successfully with no errors
Running the built image with valid env vars responds `200` on `/trpc/health.check`

steps.description: Add builder stage for shared packages and server
steps.details: In `Dockerfile`, stage `builder`: copy monorepo, run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, build `packages/schema` then `apps/server` (`tsc` output to `apps/server/dist`).

steps.description: Add builder stage for admin panel
steps.details: Stage `admin-builder`: build `apps/admin` via Vite (`vite build`), producing static assets in `apps/admin/dist`.

Stage `runner`: use a slim Node.js base image, copy `apps/server/dist`, `apps/server/node_modules` (production-only, via `pnpm install --prod`), and `apps/admin/dist` (mounted as static assets served by the server).

steps.description: Wire server to serve admin static assets
steps.details: Ensure `apps/server`'s HTTP layer (from TASK-5) serves `apps/admin/dist` as static files for all non-`/trpc` and non-`/api` routes, so a single process serves both API and panel.
In the runner stage, declare a `VOLUME` for the directory containing `DATABASE_PATH` (e.g. `/app/data`) and `EXPOSE $PORT`. Set `CMD` to start `apps/server/dist/index.js`.
Run `docker build -t systembook:dev .`, then `docker run --env-file .env.local -p 3000:3000 systembook:dev`, and confirm `curl http://localhost:3000/trpc/health.check` returns 200.

technicalNotes: Keep the final image minimal — do not include devDependencies, source TypeScript files, or the monorepo's other unbuilt packages (`connector`, `preview-kit`) unless the server needs them at runtime.
The SQLite file must live on a mounted volume, not baked into the image, so data survives container recreation.
